content.claim: Hungary's PISA sample is unusually socioeconomically advantaged (36% in top international quintile), yet Hungary has a smaller proportion of top performers (Level 5–6) than OECD average, suggesting inefficient conversion of advantaged enrollment into excellence outcomes.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Describes outcome gap but does not isolate whether gimnázium tracks cause underperformance of advantaged students or mask it
Does not indicate whether this is a relative or absolute performance failure
World Bank framing is interpretive (dual challenge) rather than causal finding
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
